title: Taeniophyllum samoense
taxonomy: Taeniophyllum samoense is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. The genus Taeniophyllum is a part of the subtribe Aeridinae. This orchid is native to the Pacific Islands and is a type of terrestrial orchid. The classification of Taeniophyllum samoense is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, and then the class Liliopsida. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Taeniophyllum samoense is a species of orchid native to Samoa, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and epiphytic growth habit. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce oxidative stress.
